title: saponaria sicula subsp. sicula
intro: Saponaria sicula subsp. sicula, also known as Sicilian soapwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to create a rich lather when mixed with water, making it a natural soap substitute. The plant is rich in saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing several health benefits such as soothing skin irritations and relieving respiratory issues. Saponaria sicula subsp. sicula can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or topical cream, and it is often used in herbal remedies to promote skin health and alleviate digestive issues.
